description: UCB1 scoring made default in dispatch_optimizer.py. 20% DARPA exploration floor implemented. Forward simulation validates behavioral change: 0 dispatches to meta in 20 rounds.
expect: UCB1 (c=1.414) + 20% cold-domain floor replaces current scoring. Score Gini decreases >30%. Coverage uniformity improves. 20-round simulation shows Gini drop.

forward_simulation.finding: UCB1 dispatches exclusively to underexplored domains for 20+ rounds. Meta (n=79) never selected. Gini improvement gradual because stock of visits (542) dilutes marginal additions.

actual.key_finding: Score uniformity != visit uniformity. Heuristic has LOWER score Gini (0.175 vs 0.300) but visits WORSENED (Gini 0.459→0.827 over 10 sessions). UCB1 concentrates scores on under-explored domains BY DESIGN, which drives uniform visits over time. The 4 rounds of heuristic patches optimized score uniformity — the wrong target. UCB1 solves visit uniformity through score NON-uniformity.

diff: Predicted Score Gini decreases >30% — WRONG, UCB1 score Gini is 71.3% HIGHER. But this is correct behavior: UCB1 achieves visit uniformity through score NON-uniformity. Did NOT predict the score-uniformity/visit-uniformity inversion. Score spread reduction confirmed (87.7%). Top-10 overlap only 3/10 — more radical than expected. Forward simulation validated: 0 meta dispatches in 20 rounds (was #1-2 under heuristic). 20% floor correctly marks 6 domains. Gini improvement gradient (-0.42%/round) modest but directionally correct.
next_steps: Re-measure visit Gini at S385 (10 sessions of UCB1-default usage)
If Gini improves >5pp, remove heuristic constants (dead code)
Thompson sampling (Tier 2) if UCB1 shows non-stationarity issues
Wire floor-protected domains into orient.py dispatch section
